spec_match.rationale: Perfectly models the comma-separated container query spec using real CSS container definitions (container-type: inline-size) and actual @container queries to drive native browser layout changes.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive with dual width sliders to resize both containers independently and watch the card stack natively, alongside a clear query matches indicator.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly matches the comma-separated query description.

edge_cases.rationale: Works natively. Falls back to normal grid layout gracefully if the browser has basic container query support but lacks the comma-separated list query parser.

references.rationale: Contains correct md links to sibling OR Breakpoints concept and MDN.

design_system.rationale: Integrates correctly with card layout, mono status lines, and flat styling.

openQuestions.title: Provide explicit feature detection fallback styling
openQuestions.detail: If the browser doesn't support comma-separated container queries, the CSS is ignored entirely, leaving both columns unstacked at narrow widths. Adding a CSS @supports block or a feature-detection JS check could alert the user or inject the duplicate-query CSS fallback dynamically to guarantee layout sanity on older browsers.

summary: A robust and native demonstration of comma-separated CSS @container query limits, with smooth resizers and highly accurate visual outcome syncing.
